content.problemStatement:
The H-index of an array is defined to be the maximum integer HHH such that there are at least HHH elements in the array which are greater than or equal to HHH.

You're given an integer array AAA and a positive integer KKK. You can do the following operation as many times as you want (possibly, none):


Pick two indices iii and jjj such that AiA_iAi​ is divisible KKK and AjA_jAj​ is not divisible by KKK; then divide AiA_iAi​ by KKK and multiply AjA_jAj​ by KKK.
Find the maximum possible H-index of AAA that can be attained after performing the above operation as many times as you like.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the size of the array and the parameter KKK as described in the statement.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, denoting the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum H-index you can achieve.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose i=5i = 5i=5 and j=1j = 1j=1, dividing A5A_5A5​ by K=2K = 2K=2 and multiplying A1A_1A1​ by 222.
Now, the array becomes [6,5,4,1,5][6, 5, 4, 1, 5][6,5,4,1,5]. There are four elements ≥4\geq 4≥4, so the H-index is 444.
It can be shown that this is optimal.
Test case 222: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=2j = 2j=2, turning AAA into [3,3][3, 3][3,3] which has H-index 222. This is optimal.
Test case 333: We cannot do any operations to multiply A1A_1A1​ and A3A_3A3​ since they are already divisible by 222.
